summary: Finished a large Ness fighter decompilation slice by replacing most of ftNess_6.s with C and also moving adjacent ftNess_4/ftNess_5 code into C. The PR switched obj_files.mk from asm objects to C objects for ftNess_4.c, ftNess_5.c, and ftNess_6.c, added many ftNess.h prototypes, centralized the lbvector include in ftNess.h, and corrected SpecialAttrs_Ness.x2240 from u32 to void* to model the stored PK Flash item GObj pointer. The matched code covers Ness down-smash yoyo callbacks, PK Fire spawn/action callbacks, and PK Flash start/charge/release animation/IASA/physics/collision paths. There were no PR body notes or review comments in the slice, so reviewer intent is not evidenced.
what_changed: Removed 959 lines of ftNess_6.s and added 695 lines of ftNess_6.c, leaving only the asm section shell in the assembly file.
Removed 384 lines from ftNess_4.s and added 243 lines to ftNess_4.c for Ness down-smash action-state handlers and callbacks, including func_8011659C, func_801168C4, and func_80116AE8.
Added 91 lines to ftNess_5.c for PK Fire-related C code, including lbl_80116B70, func_80116C94, and func_80116D04.
Switched obj_files.mk to build ftNess_4.c.o, ftNess_5.c.o, and ftNess_6.c.o instead of the corresponding asm .s.o files.
Added ftNess.h prototypes for many newly-decompiled functions from func_8011659C through lbl_80117B04, plus helper-like symbols SetPKFlashAttr and GravityDelay.
Moved the lbvector include from ftNess_3.c into ftNess.h, making Vec3 available to the new shared Ness declarations/users.
Changed struct SpecialAttrs_Ness field x2240 in fighter.h from u32 to void*, matching its use as Ness's stored PK Flash item/object pointer.
Implemented PK Flash cleanup paths that clear sa.ness.x2240 and reset OnDeath2/OnTakeDamage callbacks, plus item-owner cleanup via func_802AAA50 when present.
Implemented PK Flash start/charge/release transitions for grounded and aerial states, including raw action state IDs such as 0x15C, 0x15D, 0x15E, 0x15F, 0x160, 0x161, 0x162, and 0x163.
Implemented collision transitions between grounded and aerial PK Flash variants using ground/air helpers such as func_80082708, func_80081D0C, func_8007D5D4, and func_8007D7FC.
smart_moves: Updated obj_files.mk in the same PR as the C additions, ensuring the build actually uses the matched C objects instead of stale asm objects.
Corrected x2240 to a pointer type in fighter.h because the new C code stores, compares, clears, and passes it as a PK Flash item/GObj pointer rather than as an integer.
Centralized lbvector.h in ftNess.h after multiple Ness translation units/prototypes needed Vec3, avoiding a local include only in ftNess_3.c.
Used small helper routines such as SetPKFlashAttr and GravityDelay to express repeated PK Flash state reset and gravity-delay countdown patterns while preserving match-oriented structure.
Kept decomp.me scratch links next to functions, providing future workers with provenance for individual matched functions.
Preserved match-sensitive details such as filler stack arrays, explicit casts, raw action-state constants, and branch-shaping goto labels where needed.
decomp_lessons: When a field is used as an object/item handle, update the struct type to a pointer instead of forcing casts around an integer field; this improves C readability and matches call-site intent.
Large fighter move decompilation may require matching adjacent split files, not just the titled file; this PR finished ftNess_6.c while also moving ftNess_4 and ftNess_5 code to C.
Do not forget build-list migration: adding matched C is incomplete until obj_files.mk stops linking the corresponding asm object.
Shared declarations can force header hygiene changes; moving a Vec3 dependency into ftNess.h was needed once ftNess declarations and functions crossed translation-unit boundaries.

For fighter callbacks, model the repeated action-state callback slots explicitly, such as x21BC_callback_Accessory4, x21C0_callback_OnGiveDamage, x21DC_callback_OnTakeDamage, and x21E4_callback_OnDeath2.
For state-machine-heavy fighter code, raw action-state IDs may be retained during matching when symbolic names are not yet available; document behavior with comments rather than inventing names.
naming_conventions: New functions retain address-based names such as func_80116F94 and lbl_801171BC when no stable semantic symbol exists.

Comments annotate semantic roles with move and callback type, for example "Ness's grounded PK Fire Animation callback", "Ness's aerial PK Flash Charge IASA callback", and "Ness's Down Smash Charge Action State handler".
Temporary fighter variables commonly use fighter_gobj and fighter_data, with duplicate loads named temp_fighter or fighter_data2 when needed for matching.

Ness special attributes use descriptive field names in ftNessAttributes, such as x0_PKFLASH_TIMER1_MINCHARGEFRAMES, x4_PKFLASH_TIMER2_LOOPFRAMES, x14_FALL_ACCELERATION, x1C_LANDING_LAG, x30_PKFIRE_SPAWN_X, and xAC_SMASH_CHARGE_DURATION.
Generic fighter state variables remain offset-based but are annotated by existing field names such as x2340_stateVar1, x2344_stateVar2, x2348_stateVar3, and x234C_stateVar4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used stack filler arrays such as s32 filler[2], filler[4], filler[6], and filler[7] to reproduce stack layout/register allocation.
Used explicit integer and pointer comparisons against 0U and casts such as (s32)fighter_data->x2340_stateVar1 to shape generated code.
Used goto label block_friction in aerial PK Flash physics functions to match the shared friction tail after an optional gravity-delay branch.
Added dummy float-returning helpers return_void, return_void2, and return_void3 with a comment "Define float order for match" to influence floating constant ordering.
Duplicated local fighter pointer loads in PK Flash start animation handlers to match assembly register/data flow around item spawning.
Preserved exact floating literals and thresholds, including 0.20000000298023224f, 3.0f, 12.0f, 13.0f, 14.0f, and comparisons against 1.0f/0.0f.
Set callbacks and state variables in the same order as assembly around Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC, func_8006EBA4, ftAnim_SetAnimRate, and related helper calls.
Kept many extern declarations locally for not-yet-prototyped dependencies such as func_802AA054, func_802AAA50, func_802AA8C0, func_802AA7E4, and func_802AA7F0.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
No PR body was provided; rationale is inferred from the title, changed files, and diff only.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename Ness PK Fire or PK Flash functions from address-based lbl_/func_ names to semantic names.
Search for later struct refinements of SpecialAttrs_Ness and ftNessAttributes, especially sa.ness.x2240 and PK Flash item ownership fields.
Check whether helper symbols SetPKFlashAttr, GravityDelay, return_void, return_void2, and return_void3 were later inlined, renamed, or removed.
Look for PRs that replace raw Ness action-state IDs 0x15C-0x165 with enum constants.
Search for review or cleanup PRs touching ftNess_4.c, ftNess_5.c, or ftNess_6.c after PR 382.
